title: Highest temperature in London on July 20?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 20 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the London City Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent official forecasts from the Met Office and ensemble model runs indicate a moderating Atlantic airflow will keep London's July 20 maximum near 24–26°C after earlier July heatwaves peaked above 30°C. Key differentiators among these closely matched outcomes include the precise timing of any residual high-pressure ridge, urban heat island amplification in central London, and minor variations in cloud cover or wind speed that models currently resolve within a narrow 2°C envelope. With resolution hinging on verified observations at official stations, traders are weighting the most recent deterministic guidance against historical July variability around the 22–27°C climatological range.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ensemble forecasts from Météo-France and European models indicate a moderate high of 24–26°C for Paris on July 20, driven by a northwesterly flow and partial cloud cover that limits daytime heating. This aligns with climatological norms for mid-July, where average highs reach about 24–25°C, following an earlier heat spell that peaked near 36°C around July 7 but has since eased. Trader consensus, reflected in the 48.5% implied probability for 24°C and 25% for 25°C, incorporates these model outputs and the low likelihood of extreme deviation in the final 48-hour window. Shorter-range updates from official agencies over the next day will refine wind, insolation, and boundary-layer details that could shift the exact maximum by 1–2°C.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 20 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Short-term forecasts for Seoul point to a July 20 high near 28°C as the most likely outcome, with market-implied odds clustered tightly around 26–29°C.** Persistent monsoon (Changma) influences are delivering widespread cloud cover and scattered showers that limit daytime heating, keeping maximum temperatures from climbing much above the seasonal norm of roughly 27–30°C. Model consensus from agencies such as the Korea Meteorological Administration highlights modest variability tied to the exact timing and coverage of rain, which can suppress peaks by 1–2°C on overcast days or allow brief clearing that pushes readings toward 29°C. Historical July data show similar conditions produce daily highs averaging near 28°C with a standard deviation of about 2°C. Traders are therefore weighting the narrow spread between 27°C and 29°C most heavily, while awaiting the next official model runs that will refine precipitation and insolation details before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from major centers show Ankara under persistent high pressure on July 20, producing clear skies, light winds, and strong daytime radiative heating that support daily maxima near 34–35 °C. Ensemble spreads place the most probable outcomes in this narrow range, reflected in the market’s near-even split between those two bins. Subtle differences in boundary-layer moisture, exact timing of peak insolation, and local urban heat effects create the remaining uncertainty between 34 °C and 35 °C, while cooler or hotter outliers remain low-probability given current guidance. Updated model runs and official Turkish Meteorological Service briefings over the next 48 hours will likely tighten the consensus ahead of market resolution.

Mid-July places Wellington in peak Southern Hemisphere winter, when climatological daily maxima average 12–13°C under typical westerly flow and marine moderation from the Tasman Sea and Cook Strait. The latest extended forecast from official sources projects a 54°F (12.2°C) high on July 20 under mostly cloudy skies with light winds around 8 mph, consistent with near-average or slightly below-average conditions noted in seasonal outlooks. Model consensus shows limited potential for warmer air advection or clear-sky radiative warming that would push readings to 13–14°C, while cold outbreaks capable of dropping maxima to 11°C or lower remain possible but less favored in the immediate run. Recent days have featured highs in the 13–14°C range, but the approaching pattern favors stabilization around the 12°C threshold. Updated model guidance closer to the date will be the key variable for traders, as small shifts in cloud cover or wind direction can alter the realized maximum within the narrow winter range.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment for Shanghai’s July 20 maximum temperature reflects model consensus around the low-to-mid 30s Celsius amid typical mid-July conditions.** Official and commercial forecasts (AccuWeather monthly guidance, timeanddate.com, BBC, and Chinese meteorological summaries) currently project daily highs of 31–34 °C for the 19–21 July period, with July 20 specifically centered near 31–32 °C under partly cloudy skies and isolated thunderstorms. These values align with the post-plum-rain transition, when monsoon moisture lingers, increasing cloud cover and convective activity that caps daytime heating despite seasonal solar input.

Key differentiating factors include the strength and timing of any passing short-wave troughs or sea-breeze effects, which can suppress or enhance maximum temperatures by 1–2 °C, and the precise evolution of boundary-layer moisture that influences both instability and insolation. Historical climatology shows July averages near 34 °C but with high day-to-day variability (±3–4 °C) driven by these mesoscale features. Because the market resolves on the single official highest reading—most likely the China Meteorological Administration station value—traders weigh ensemble spread in global and regional models, noting that even modest increases in cloud persistence or earlier convective initiation could shift the outcome from 34 °C toward 32 °C or lower. With resolution only two days away, the next 12–24 h of updated model runs and observational trends will likely tighten the distribution around the current 32–34 °C cluster.

eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore's tropical climate and ongoing southwest monsoon drive the tight clustering of market-implied odds around 31–33 °C for the July 20 daily maximum, with traders weighing the balance between persistent high humidity and variable afternoon convection. Official outlooks from the Meteorological Service Singapore highlight typical maxima of 33–34 °C under the current monsoon flow, tempered by short-duration thundery showers that reduce insolation and surface heating on many days. El Niño-related warming trends add a modest upward bias relative to long-term July averages near 31 °C, yet model consensus shows sensitivity to cloud-cover timing and localized rainfall—factors that can suppress peaks by 1–2 °C or allow brief exceedances above 34 °C. Updated regional forecasts and satellite observations over the next 48 hours will likely refine these probabilities ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stable high-pressure patterns over northern Italy are anchoring the market-implied odds near 30–31 °C for Milan’s July 20 maximum, with ensemble guidance from major models showing limited day-to-day variability and no significant frontal passage expected before the target date. The Po Valley’s characteristic subsidence warming, light winds, and urban heat-island effects favor readings in this narrow band, consistent with climatological July averages of 29–32 °C and recent observations showing daytime peaks of 30–33 °C. Traders are weighting the latest short-range runs that keep the most likely outcome at or just above 30 °C while assigning low probability to outliers above 32 °C or below 29 °C. Updated model cycles and any last-minute adjustments to boundary-layer moisture remain the key near-term catalysts that could shift the distribution before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from agencies including the Met Office and BBC project Wuhan's July 20 maximum near 32–33°C under partly cloudy conditions with light southerly winds and isolated shower chances, aligning with the market's leading implied probabilities for the 33°C and 34°C-or-higher brackets. In Wuhan's subtropical monsoon climate, peak July insolation typically supports highs in the 32–35°C range, though boundary-layer humidity, timing of any convective clouds, and precise steering of warm air masses introduce spread across ensemble runs. Recent short-range updates have kept consensus anchored near these values rather than the monthly climatological potential for 35°C-plus episodes, with traders monitoring final model iterations and official station observations through the weekend for resolution signals on the exact daily peak.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Forecast models and regional heatwave conditions currently anchor trader sentiment around 38–39°C for Chengdu’s July 20 high.** Short-range guidance from sources such as timeanddate and regional outlooks points to sunny skies and highs of 37–38°C (99–100°F) on the 19th–20th, while broader July climatology and recent Sichuan basin readings—including Chongqing’s 40°C reading earlier in the month—support the slim edge for 39°C. Chengdu’s enclosed topography and high humidity limit nocturnal cooling and can amplify daytime maxima under clear, subsiding air. Model ensembles show modest spread tied to exact cloud cover and wind timing, keeping 37°C and 40°C as close secondary outcomes. With resolution less than 48 hours away, the next updated runs from national meteorological centers will likely tighten probabilities further.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 20 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders are closely divided between 29–31°C for Shenzhen’s July 20 maximum because short-range models show scattered thunderstorms and persistent cloud cover that will limit solar heating in the city’s humid subtropical environment. Frequent afternoon convection typical of the East Asian monsoon season reduces insolation and promotes evaporative cooling, often capping daytime peaks 1–3°C below clear-sky climatology near 32°C. Key variables include the exact timing and coverage of showers, local sea-breeze strength, and urban heat retention; stronger or earlier storms favor the lower end of the range while partial clearing could allow 31–32°C. Official guidance from the China Meteorological Administration and global ensembles will refine these details over the next 48 hours, keeping implied probabilities tightly clustered around the most likely outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-range forecast models show Istanbul under stable high pressure with light northerly Etesian winds and minimal cloud cover through July 20, favoring daytime highs near the July climatological average of 28–30 °C. Recent regional warmth in 2025–26 provides a slightly elevated baseline, yet model spread remains wide enough that traders assign comparable probabilities across the narrow 28–31 °C band ahead of updated runs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Busan’s coastal location under the influence of the North Pacific High is sustaining above-average July highs amid ongoing heatwave advisories from the Korea Meteorological Administration. Recent model consensus points to daytime maxima near 31–33 °C on July 20, with the slight edge to 32 °C reflecting limited cloud cover and weak sea-breeze moderation expected that day. Monsoon (Jangma) moisture and any late-day showers remain the chief variables that could cap the peak one degree lower or allow brief clearing to push it higher. Historical July climatology for Busan shows mean highs of 27–29 °C, so the current pattern represents a clear positive anomaly driven by persistent subsidence and high humidity that reduces nocturnal cooling. Updated KMA and global model runs issued in the next 48 hours will refine the exact threshold.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model guidance from sources like ECMWF and regional outlooks points to a maximum of 20–22°C in Amsterdam on July 20 under a typical mid-summer pattern of variable Atlantic flow, moderate onshore winds, and patchy cloud or light rain that limits peak insolation. This aligns with climatological averages near 22°C while explaining the tight clustering of market probabilities around 19–21°C, as small shifts in cloud timing or frontal passage could trim or add 1–2°C. Marine air influence from the North Sea further caps extremes, with low likelihood of the warmer 23–24°C outcomes absent a strong ridge build-up. Updated short-range runs and KNMI briefings over the next 48 hours will refine these thresholds ahead of market resolution.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 20 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Helsinki Vantaa Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fi/vantaa/EFHK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Kuala Lumpur's July 20 high centers on the 31–33°C range, consistent with long-term July climatology of 31–32°C average maxima under the Southwest Monsoon and typical equatorial humidity. Recent model guidance from sources such as AccuWeather projects daily highs of 30.8–33°C with minimal day-to-day variability through late July, supporting the 46% implied probability on 32°C as the most likely outcome. Historical observations show occasional peaks to 34–35°C during drier spells, reflected in the secondary 24.5% and 12% probabilities on 33°C and 34°C, while lower readings remain unlikely absent unusual cloud cover or rainfall. Updated short-range forecasts in the next 48 hours will refine these odds ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast models from sources like the China Meteorological Administration and international ensembles point to Guangzhou experiencing partly cloudy to overcast conditions with scattered thunderstorms on July 20, keeping peak temperatures clustered around 32–34 °C under the influence of the East Asian monsoon trough and subtropical ridge positioning. High humidity (typically 70–80 %) and frequent afternoon convection limit insolation and evaporative cooling, narrowing the range between 32 °C (favored if storms develop early) and 34 °C (possible under brief clear spells). Historical July maxima average near 33 °C, but daily variability from steering flow and moisture convergence creates the tight market spread, with lower outcomes (≤31 °C) requiring stronger or more persistent rainfall that current guidance does not strongly support. Updated model runs and official briefings through July 19 will refine timing of any convective activity ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast guidance from models and agencies points to a high near 29°C in Qingdao on July 20 under mostly cloudy skies with possible showers, consistent with the market's highest-implied probability for that outcome. Mid-July climatology for the coastal Shandong location features average daily maxima of 27–28°C moderated by the Yellow Sea, and current guidance shows limited intensification potential from weak steering flow and high humidity. Key variables include cloud cover and precipitation timing, which can suppress peaks by 1–2°C, versus clearer intervals that could allow 30–31°C readings; ensemble spreads remain wide only two days ahead. Traders are weighting these near-term model runs heavily, with lower probabilities assigned to extremes given the narrow historical range and typical marine influence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see 32–33°C as the most likely peak for Manila on July 20 because the southwest monsoon continues to deliver persistent cloud cover, high humidity, and scattered afternoon showers that limit solar heating and evaporative cooling. Typical July climatology from PAGASA and long-term observations places average daily maxima near 31°C, but brief breaks in convection or stronger easterly flow can allow brief spikes to 33°C, while heavier rain suppresses readings toward 31°C. Model consensus emphasizes these moisture-driven variables over the next 48 hours, with the tight spread between leading outcomes reflecting uncertainty in exact timing of convective activity rather than any major shift in the broader pattern.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent monsoon activity across northern India, including ongoing spells of rain and thundershowers in Uttar Pradesh, has moderated daytime heating in Lucknow through increased cloud cover and evaporative cooling. Official monitoring indicates typical July highs near 33°C, yet frequent precipitation and high humidity—common during the active phase of the southwest monsoon—tend to cap peaks at 30–32°C. Model consensus from extended-range forecasts shows subtle differences in the timing and intensity of showers expected around July 20, which directly influence solar radiation and the resulting maximum temperature. With leading market outcomes clustered tightly at 30–31°C, traders are pricing in this narrow band of forecast uncertainty rather than a single deterministic value, as small shifts in rainfall timing can swing the daily high by 1–2°C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from agencies including the Met Office and BBC indicate daytime highs in Buenos Aires around 11–13 °C on July 20 amid typical mid-winter conditions, with southerly winds and periods of cloud and light rain limiting solar heating. The closely matched market probabilities for 13 °C and 14 °C reflect model uncertainty in the precise arrival and strength of a passing frontal system that could either suppress or allow modest afternoon warming. Key differentiating factors include surface pressure gradients steering cooler maritime air, the extent of low-level cloud cover reducing daytime insolation, and local urban heat-island effects that can add 1–2 °C relative to rural stations. Historical July highs average near 15 °C, so any delay in the front or clearer skies would support the higher outcomes while a stronger cold surge would favor 12 °C or below. Updated model runs tomorrow morning will likely refine these probabilities ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from Environment Canada and private sources currently converge on a Toronto high near 26–27 °C for July 20, driving the market’s leading probabilities for those outcomes. eventMetadata.context_description: Recent National Weather Service and AccuWeather guidance points to a mostly sunny Monday with highs near 90–92 °F under continued high pressure, aligning with the market’s heaviest weighting on the 92–93 °F bin (41 %). July climatology for Atlanta shows a 30-year normal high of ~89 °F, yet persistent ridging and above-average soil moisture deficits have supported readings 2–4 °F above normal through mid-month. Model consensus remains tight on minimal cloud cover and light northwest flow, limiting convective cooling and keeping the upper end of the distribution around 94–95 °F (20.5 %). Updated 00Z runs show little shift from yesterday’s guidance, leaving only modest probability mass below 90 °F or above 95 °F.

eventMetadata.context_description: A strengthening heat dome and upper-level ridge over Texas are driving the market's 54.5% implied probability on a 96-97°F high for Houston on July 20. National Weather Service and AccuWeather guidance place the expected maximum in the mid- to upper 90s under mostly sunny skies, consistent with July climatology at Bush Intercontinental where the normal high is 95°F. Limited moisture and light winds should allow full daytime heating without significant cloud cover or seabreeze moderation, while overnight lows near 76-78°F maintain elevated heat indices. Short-term model consensus shows little day-to-day variability through the period, keeping the 94-99°F range dominant in trader pricing ahead of the final NWS forecast update.

eventMetadata.context_description: Marine layer moderation and persistent onshore flow from the Pacific are the dominant factors capping trader expectations for Los Angeles International Airport observations on July 20, favoring the closely matched 78-79°F and 80-81°F bins. Recent National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model consensus indicate a modest warming trend from mid-July coastal maxima near 74-77°F, yet afternoon sea breezes and low-level stratus are expected to limit peak heating. This aligns with climatological July averages around 81-86°F while explaining lower probabilities for 84°F-plus outcomes. Key variables differentiating the leading ranges include slight differences in forecast boundary-layer mixing and exact timing of any marine push, with new model runs and morning soundings likely to refine sentiment before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market-implied odds favor 72-73°F as the leading outcome for San Francisco’s July 20 high, reflecting trader consensus on a moderately suppressed marine layer that permits modest afternoon warming under persistent high pressure. The city’s characteristic summer temperature inversion—driven by cool Pacific waters and the California Current—typically caps highs near the 68°F July climatological mean, yet recent model runs show reduced onshore flow and a shallower layer depth allowing peaks in the low 70s. Stronger La Niña conditions this season have reinforced the marine layer overall, creating uncertainty around exact timing of any sea-breeze surge or fog clearance that could shift the high by a degree or two. National Weather Service updates through the evening of July 19 will refine these thresholds ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader sentiment for Mexico City's July 20 high centers on the narrow gap between 26°C and 27°C implied probabilities, reflecting model consensus around slightly above the long-term July average of ~24°C.** Official climatology from sources like AccuWeather and WeatherSpark places typical highs at 22–25°C due to the city's 2,240 m elevation and July rainy-season dynamics, where afternoon cloud cover, scattered thunderstorms, and evaporative cooling from rainfall often cap daytime peaks. Recent short-range forecasts and ensemble runs showing reduced precipitation or clearer morning conditions have nudged expectations upward, with traders weighing variables such as dew-point levels, wind patterns from the northeast, and potential model spread in maximum temperature guidance. Historical analogs indicate occasional excursions to 26–27°C when subtropical moisture is limited, while sustained monsoon activity tends to hold readings nearer 24–25°C; resolution hinges on the precise observed maximum at official stations amid these competing influences.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Typical July conditions in Panama City's wet season produce daily highs near 30°C, with the closely matched 29–30°C market outcomes reflecting uncertainty over afternoon convective cloud cover and shower timing. Abundant solar insolation at low latitude drives rapid morning warming, yet frequent ITCZ-related thunderstorms and high humidity often cap peaks through reduced insolation and evaporative cooling. Sea-breeze influences from the Pacific and variable wind patterns add further variability, while climatological data show July averages of 30–31°C with a narrow observed range. Short-term model runs and satellite trends on July 19 will likely refine expectations for July 20 resolution.
